questions.text: What Jinja2 syntax renders a variable value in a template?
questions.explanation: Jinja2 uses double curly braces {{ }} to render variable values. Single braces {} are Python dicts, $ is shell syntax, and [[ ]] is not Jinja2. This is the same syntax used everywhere in Ansible for variable interpolation.

questions.text: Which Jinja2 filter provides a fallback value when a variable is undefined?
questions.explanation: The 'default' filter provides a fallback value: {{ nginx_port | default(80) }}. If nginx_port is undefined, 80 is used. You can also use | default('value', true) to treat empty strings and false as undefined.

questions.text: What module deploys a Jinja2 template file to a managed node?
questions.explanation: ansible.builtin.template processes a .j2 file using Jinja2, substituting variables, and copies the result to the destination. Unlike ansible.builtin.copy which transfers files verbatim, template evaluates the Jinja2 expressions first.

questions.text: What does the 'register' keyword do in an Ansible task?

options.text: Registers the task with Ansible Tower
Saves the task's return value into a variable
Logs the task output to a file
Marks the task as a handler

questions.explanation: The 'register' keyword saves a task's output (return value) into a variable. The registered variable contains fields like stdout, stderr, rc (return code), and changed. Use it to make decisions based on command output or module results.

questions.text: What is the correct Jinja2 syntax for a for loop in a template?
questions.explanation: Jinja2 uses {% %} for control structures like for loops and if statements. {{ }} is for variable output, and {# #} is for comments. So a for loop is {% for item in list %}...{% endfor %}.

options.text: Gathers system facts from managed nodes
Creates or overrides a variable for the remainder of the play
Sets a fact in the Ansible database permanently
Defines a variable in a role's defaults/main.yml

questions.explanation: set_fact creates a host variable that persists for the duration of the play (and is accessible by subsequent plays). It's useful for setting computed values derived from registered output or complex expressions, making them available as simple variable names.

questions.text: Which filter converts a list to a comma-separated string?
questions.explanation: The join filter concatenates list items with a separator: {{ packages | join(', ') }}. This is useful in templates for generating comma-separated values, space-separated arguments, or any delimiter-separated strings.

questions.text: How do you comment out text in a Jinja2 template so it doesn't appear in output?
questions.explanation: Jinja2 comments use {# #} syntax and are completely removed from output. Regular # comments in YAML/config are passed through as-is. Use {# #} when you want developer notes in the template that shouldn't appear in the generated file.

questions.text: What variable contains the hostname of a managed node, gathered as a fact?
questions.explanation: ansible_hostname is a fact containing the hostname of the managed node as reported by the OS. inventory_hostname is the name used in the inventory file (may be different). ansible_host is the connection address. ansible_fqdn has the fully qualified domain name.

questions.text: What does the 'validate' parameter do in ansible.builtin.template?

options.text: Validates the Jinja2 syntax before rendering
Runs a command to validate the generated file before placing it
Checks the file's MD5 checksum after deployment
Verifies that all template variables are defined

questions.explanation: The validate parameter runs a command (with %s as the file path placeholder) to validate the generated file before placing it at the destination. Example: validate: '/usr/sbin/nginx -t -c %s'. If validation fails, Ansible does not replace the destination file.
